questions.q: B. R. Ambedkar is best known as the principal architect of which document?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar chaired the drafting committee and is regarded as the chief architect of the Indian Constitution.

questions.q: What does the 'B. R.' in B. R. Ambedkar's name stand for?
questions.explanation: His full name was Bhimrao Ramji Ambedkar.

questions.q: Ambedkar dedicated his life primarily to the rights of which section of society?
options: The depressed classes and those facing untouchability
questions.explanation: Ambedkar championed the rights of the depressed classes and led the struggle against untouchability.

questions.q: What position did Ambedkar hold in independent India's first cabinet?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar became independent India's first Law Minister.

questions.q: By which affectionate title is Ambedkar widely remembered?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar is popularly and affectionately known as Babasaheb.

questions.q: Ambedkar was trained in which profession, among others?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar was a jurist and economist, with advanced training in law and economics.

questions.q: Ambedkar chaired which key committee of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: In which religion did Ambedkar take refuge late in his life?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar embraced Buddhism in 1956, leading a mass conversion of his followers.

questions.q: Ambedkar campaigned strongly against which social evil within Hindu society?
questions.explanation: He waged a lifelong campaign against untouchability and caste discrimination.

questions.q: Ambedkar's vision for the depressed classes emphasised which trio of ideals?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar repeatedly invoked liberty, equality and fraternity as the guiding ideals of a just society.

questions.q: In which year was B. R. Ambedkar born?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar was born on 14 April 1891 at Mhow in central India.

questions.q: At which American university did Ambedkar earn advanced degrees?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar studied at Columbia University in New York, earning a doctorate there.

questions.q: Ambedkar also studied in Britain at which institution, where he qualified for the bar and economics?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar studied at the London School of Economics and was called to the bar at Gray's Inn.

questions.q: In 1927 Ambedkar led a satyagraha at Mahad to assert the right of the depressed classes to do what?
options: Draw water from a public tank
questions.explanation: The Mahad Satyagraha of 1927 asserted the right of the depressed classes to take water from the public Chavdar tank.

questions.q: Which newspaper did Ambedkar start in 1920 to advance his cause?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar founded the periodical Mooknayak, meaning 'leader of the voiceless,' in 1920.

questions.q: Ambedkar founded which organisation in 1924 for the welfare of the depressed classes?
questions.explanation: He founded the Bahishkrit Hitakarini Sabha in 1924 to promote education and welfare among the depressed classes.

questions.q: Ambedkar represented the depressed classes at which series of London talks in 1930-32?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar attended all three Round Table Conferences as a representative of the depressed classes.

questions.q: The 1932 Communal Award proposed what for the depressed classes, which Ambedkar initially supported?
questions.explanation: The Communal Award granted separate electorates to the depressed classes, a provision Ambedkar initially backed.

questions.q: The 1932 agreement between Ambedkar and Gandhi over electorates is known as which pact?
questions.explanation: The Poona Pact of 1932 replaced separate electorates with reserved seats for the depressed classes.

questions.q: On what date in 1956 did Ambedkar publicly convert to Buddhism?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar embraced Buddhism in a mass ceremony at Nagpur on 14 October 1956.

questions.q: In which city did Ambedkar lead the historic mass conversion to Buddhism in 1956?
questions.explanation: The mass conversion took place at Nagpur, at a site later known as Deekshabhoomi.

questions.q: Ambedkar served as a member of the Viceroy's Executive Council during the war years, holding which portfolio?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar served as Labour member of the Viceroy's Executive Council in the early 1940s.

questions.q: Ambedkar resigned as Law Minister in 1951 partly over the stalling of which reform measure?
questions.explanation: He resigned in 1951 in part over delays to the Hindu Code Bill aimed at reforming Hindu personal law.

questions.q: Which influential work did Ambedkar prepare critiquing the caste system, originally intended as a speech he was never allowed to deliver?
questions.explanation: 'Annihilation of Caste' was an undelivered 1936 address that Ambedkar published as a powerful critique of caste.

questions.q: Ambedkar founded which political party in 1936 to mobilise workers and the depressed classes?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar founded the Independent Labour Party in 1936 to contest elections in Bombay province.

questions.q: Ambedkar's economic scholarship included a notable study of what?
options: The problem of the rupee and Indian currency
questions.explanation: His economic scholarship included 'The Problem of the Rupee', analysing Indian currency and exchange.

questions.q: In 1930 Ambedkar led another temple-related satyagraha demanding entry rights at which temple?
questions.explanation: The Kalaram Temple Entry Satyagraha at Nashik in 1930 demanded the right of the depressed classes to enter the temple.

questions.q: Ambedkar's final and posthumously published book on his adopted faith was titled what?
questions.explanation: 'The Buddha and His Dhamma' was Ambedkar's major work on Buddhism, published after his death.

questions.q: In which year did B. R. Ambedkar die?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar died on 6 December 1956, weeks after his conversion to Buddhism.

questions.q: India's highest civilian honour was conferred on Ambedkar posthumously in 1990; what is that award?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar was posthumously awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian honour, in 1990.
